2606.10380.title: Expert-Level Crisis Detection in Mental Health Conversations
2606.10380.paper_summary: Real-world crisis intervention is inherently conversational, yet existing research largely focuses on static texts.Real-world crisis intervention is inherently conversational, yet existing research largely focuses on static texts. When applied to multi-turn dialogues, current models exhibit significant performance degradation, struggling to track risk signals that emerge as context evolves. To address this gap, we introduce CRADLE-Dialogue, a clinician-annotated benchmark for turn-level crisis detection in conversational settings. The dataset features 600 dialogues with multi-label annotations across clinically grounded risks, including suicide ideation, self-harm, and child abuse, distinguishing past from ongoing risk. We further propose an Alert-Confirm evaluation protocol that distinguishes early warning signals (Alert) from turns where a specific crisis becomes explicitly identifiable (Confirm), reflecting the clinical need to intervene before risk becomes explicit. Experiments show that identifying when risk emerges is much harder than recognizing that it exists: models achieve only mid-40% to high-60% Micro F1. Additionally, we release a synthetic training corpus and a 32B-parameter model that substantially outperforms existing open-source models and achieves competitive or superior results against proprietary models across turn-level, dialogue-level, and confirm-only evaluation settings.
2606.10380.paper_summary_zh: 現實世界的危機干預本質上是對話性的，但現有研究主要集中在靜態文本上。當應用於多輪對話時，當前模型顯示出顯著的性能下降，難以追蹤隨著上下文演變而出現的風險信號。為了解決這一問題，我們引入了CRADLE-Dialogue，一個臨床醫生註釋的基準，用於對話環境中的輪次級危機檢測。該數據集包含600個對話，並針對臨床相關風險進行多標籤註釋，包括自殺意念、自我傷害和兒童虐待，區分過去的風險和正在進行的風險。我們進一步提出了一種警報-確認評估協議，區分早期警告信號（警報）和特定危機變得明確可識別的輪次（確認），反映了在風險變得明確之前進行干預的臨床需求。實驗表明，識別風險何時出現比識別風險是否存在要困難得多：模型的微觀F1僅達到中40%到高60%的水平。此外，我們發布了一個合成訓練語料庫和一個32B參數的模型，該模型在輪次級、對話級和僅確認評估設置中大幅超越現有的開源模型，並在與專有模型的比較中取得競爭或更優的結果。
2606.10380.author: Grace Byun et.al.
2606.10380.authors: Grace Byun, Abigail Lott, Rebecca Lipschutz, Sean T. Minton, Elizabeth A. Stinson, Jinho D. Choi
